docs.title: Data Quality Validation Platform
docs.text: A reusable Python application that automates dataset validation, replacing manual Excel-based inspection with a structured validation pipeline and detailed reporting.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/devminda/data-validator.git
cd data-validator
pip install uv
uv sync --extra dev --extra docs
uv run pre-commit install

docs.title: Running Locally
docs.text:
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

API: http://localhost:8000 Interactive docs: http://localhost:8000/docs ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text: uv run pytest

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
.
├── app/
│   ├── config/             # column definitions, dataset mappings, constants
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── data_loader/    # base loader and CSV/JSON/Excel implementations
│   │   └── data_validator/ # base validator, registry, and all validators
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── pipelines/      # validation pipeline orchestration
│   │   └── report_generator/ # CLI and base report generators
│   └── main.py             # app entrypoint
├── examples/               # example usage scripts
├── notebooks/              # Jupyter notebooks — exploration & analysis
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/
│   └── integration/
├── docs/                   # MkDocs pages
├── Dockerfile
└── pyproject.toml          # all tool config lives here

docs.title: Branch strategy
docs.text:
• main — protected; requires CI to pass and 1 review to merge
  • feature/<name> — all work happens here

docs.title: Daily workflow
docs.text:
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/your-task
# do work
git add .
git commit -m "feat: description"   # pre-commit hooks fire here
git push origin feature/your-task
# open PR on GitHub

docs.title: Code standards (hard blocks on CI)
docs.text: Tool Purpose black Formatting isort Import order mypy Type checking pytest Tests + 70% coverage

docs.title: Adding a new endpoint
docs.text:
1. Create a schema in src/app/schemas/
  2. Create a service in src/app/services/ (business logic)
  3. Create an endpoint in src/app/api/v1/endpoints/
  4. Register it in src/app/api/v1/router.py
  5. Add tests in tests/

docs.title: Project Name: Data Quality Validation Platform
1. Background
docs.text:
Our company processes datasets from multiple clients every day. These datasets are received before being loaded into our central data warehouse.

Currently, analysts manually inspect every dataset to identify common data quality issues before the data enters downstream systems. This process is repetitive, time‑consuming, and prone to human error.

We need a Python application that automates this validation process while remaining flexible enough to support future clients and new validation requirements.

docs.title: 2. Business Problem
docs.text:
Poor quality data creates downstream issues including:

  • Incorrect reports
  • Failed ETL jobs
  • Invalid customer records
  • Financial inconsistencies
  • Increased manual correction effort

These issues must be identified before the data enters our systems.

docs.title: 3. Objective
docs.text:
Develop a reusable validation application capable of inspecting incoming datasets and producing a detailed validation report.

The application should be easy to extend as new clients and validation rules are introduced.

docs.title: 4. Current Process
docs.text:
Client
   ↓
CSV File
   ↓
Analyst opens Excel
   ↓
Checks data manually
   ↓
Finds issues
   ↓
Emails client
   ↓
Receives corrected file
   ↓
Loads into database

docs.title: 5. Desired Workflow
docs.text:
Client
   ↓
Dataset
   ↓
Validation Application
   ↓
Validation Report
   ↓
Approved  OR  Rejected

docs.title: Dataset Loading
docs.text:
The application shall:

  • Read datasets from disk
  • Process datasets of varying sizes
  • Handle multiple datasets independently

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
The application shall execute a series of validation rules against each dataset.

Example checks include (but are not limited to):

  • Missing values
  • Duplicate records
  • Invalid dates
  • Negative numeric values
  • Values outside accepted ranges
  • Empty strings
  • Invalid categorical values

The application should continue executing remaining validations even if one validation fails unexpectedly.

docs.title: Reporting
docs.text:
The application shall generate a report containing:

  • Dataset name
  • Validation performed
  • Validation result
  • Number of records affected
  • Details of affected rows
  • Overall validation summary

docs.title: Extensibility
docs.text: The application should allow new validation rules to be introduced with minimal modification to the existing codebase.

docs.title: Reliable
docs.text: A single validation failure should not terminate the entire validation process.

docs.title: Maintainable
docs.text: The codebase should be understandable and easy to extend by another developer.

docs.title: Scalable
docs.text: Datasets may range from a few hundred records to several million. The application should remain performant.

docs.title: Reusable
docs.text: The validation engine should work for different datasets without being rewritten.

docs.title: 8. Sample Datasets
docs.text:
Initial datasets include:

  • Human Resources
  • Banking
  • Online Orders
  • Inventory
  • Student Records

Each dataset may have a different schema.

docs.title: Version 2
docs.text:
Support for:

  • Excel files
  • JSON files

docs.title: Version 3
docs.text:
Support for:

  • Database tables
  • Cloud storage

docs.title: Version 4
docs.text:
Support for:

  • Scheduled validation jobs
  • Email notifications
  • Validation history
  • Dashboard

docs.title: Version 5
docs.text:
Support for:

  • User‑defined validation rules
  • Rule configuration through a configuration file
  • Plugin‑based extensions

docs.title: 10. Constraints
docs.text:
The application must:

  • Run on Python 3.x
  • Be executable from the command line
  • Produce clear error messages
  • Handle invalid datasets gracefully

No specific Python libraries or frameworks are mandated.

docs.title: 11. Deliverables
docs.text:
The final solution should include:

  • Source code
  • Documentation
  • Example datasets
  • Unit tests
  • Instructions for execution

docs.title: 12. Acceptance Criteria
docs.text:
The project will be considered complete when:

  • Multiple datasets can be validated
  • Validation results are clearly reported
  • New validation rules can be incorporated without major redesign
  • The application remains stable when validations encounter unexpected data
  • Documentation is sufficient for another developer to continue the project
